title: Presenting a Findings-Based Recommendation — Evidence, Not Opinion
verdict: All checks pass — reading is within word-count budget, cites primary resource throughout, no forward references, fully faithful to corpus, beginner-appropriate with concrete-first explanations and bold-term definitions, well-formatted with no walls of text, and strictly scoped to the topic headline.

format_discipline.detail: No frontmatter. reading.meta.json present and parses with all required fields. Exactly 6 fixed sections in correct order: Overview, Key Concepts, Worked Example, In Practice, Key Takeaways, References.

length_budget.detail: 1982 words (within 1800–2200). At 200 wpm = 9.91 min (within 9–11 min target).

citations.detail: Primary resource [1] (ReadWriteThink) cited ≥10 times across all sections. [2] and [3] cited multiple times. Bibliography lists all three references matching inline markers. No orphans in either direction.

no_forward_references.detail: No references to topics beyond 4.8, future modules, assessments, or post-sequence concepts. The corpus contained an A2 essay reference in sections 4 and 10; this correctly does not appear in the reading.

faithfulness.detail: All 10 sampled assertions trace directly to corpus sections 4–8: opinion/evidence distinction, three-part structure, score translation format, findings summary table (exact numbers), trade-off definition, limitations list, three common weaknesses tables, and real-world patterns. Zero unsupported assertions.

beginner_readability.detail: All 8 new terms (opinion, evidence statement, findings-based recommendation, findings summary, evidence anchor, recommendation statement, trade-off, limitations) are bold-defined at first appearance. No paragraph introduces 3+ new terms at once. Concepts are introduced with concrete scenarios before abstractions throughout. Zero hedging phrases detected.

formatting.detail: No paragraph exceeds 5 sentences. Procedures in Key Concepts are rendered as numbered-step lists. Worked Example uses bold step labels (structurally equivalent to numbered list). Inline concrete examples appear throughout Key Concepts sections — not deferred to Worked Example alone. All definitions use bold-term / plain-meaning shape.

assessment_references.detail: No assessment IDs, due weeks, or grade weights appear in reading.md.

scope.detail: Reading teaches exactly: evidence-based recommendation, findings summary, evidence anchor, recommendation statement, trade-off (evaluation context), evaluation limitations, and three common weaknesses. All are declared new concepts for 4.8. No downstream concepts introduced. No advanced material beyond the topic headline.

gold_example_comparison.note: No gold example exists for topic 4.8 specifically. Compared against the written standards in artifact-mix.json and the reading-critic SKILL.md. This reading meets or exceeds those standards on all dimensions: correct section structure, bold-term definitions, concrete-first ordering, inline examples, appropriate sentence length, and scope discipline.
